headline: Housing Stocks Are in Depression Mode. Whirlpool Down 81%, Lennar Crashed 54% While the S&P 500 Soars.
summary: The S&P 500 is at fresh highs while a giant chunk of the real economy sits in a deep freeze. On a recent episode of The Compound and Friends, Michael Batnick and Josh Brown spent a segment unpacking why housing-adjacent stocks have collapsed even as home prices across major metros stay stable. The distinction matters: ... Housing Stocks Are in Depression Mode. Whirlpool Down 81%, Lennar Crashed 54% While the S&P 500 Soars.

headline: Is It Time To Revisit Home Depot (HD) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
summary: Wondering if Home Depot at around US$310 a share still offers value, or if most of the opportunity is already priced in. The stock has been under pressure recently, with the share price down 1.6% over the last week, 8.0% over the last month, 10.2% year to date and 14.8% over the past year. The 3 year return sits at 18.6% and the 5 year return at 10.8%. Recent coverage has focused on how Home Depot is positioned within US home improvement spending, with investors weighing housing market...
